title: Smoky Norwegian Vegetable Gratin
description: A smoky, crunchy, and romantic Norwegian dish featuring vegetable stock cube. This gratin is perfect for a cozy dinner or special occasion.
instructions: In a large hot stone plate, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced red onions and cook for 3-4 minutes, until caramelized.
Add the Brussels sprouts and cook for 5 minutes, until tender.
In a separate saucepan, make a bechamel sauce by melting 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, whisking constantly.
Slowly add the milk, whisking continuously.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until thickened.
Remove from heat and stir in the grated cheddar cheese until melted.
In a separate bowl, mix the cooked carrots with vegetable stock cube, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
In the hot stone plate, create a layer of the cooked Brussels sprouts mixture.
Top with a layer of the carrot mixture, then a layer of the bechamel sauce.
Repeat the layers two more times, ending with a layer of bechamel sauce on top.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Let the dish rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.
